summary: postinstall reverse shell to hardcoded remote host
details: date-format-utils-xz ships a postinstall.js that opens a TCP connection to the hardcoded remote host 8.135.48.40:4444 and pipes /bin/sh stdio through the socket, with `bash -i` /dev/tcp and python3 pty fallbacks. On failure of all shell-drop methods, it beacons to http://8.135.48.40/shell/failed and /shell/error via http.get to confirm reachability. Because this runs from scripts.postinstall, a plain `npm install` gives the operator of 8.135.48.40 interactive shell access on the installer's host. The package's index.js is a trivial formatDate stub, so the library functionality is a decoy for the reverse-shell payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
